evidence_reg: If the controller does not take action on the request of the data subject, the controller shall inform the data subject without delay and at the latest within one month of receipt of the request of the reasons for not taking action and on the possibility of lodging a complaint with a supervisory authority and seeking a judicial remedy.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA mentions notification of data breaches, while the REG concerns informing a data subject about inaction on a request; these are different constraints and do not provide a quote-supported direct mismatch.

evidence_reg: Where personal data have not been obtained from the data subject, the controller shall provide the data subject with the following information:
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA refers generally to notification of data breaches, whereas the REG requires provision of information when data are not obtained from the data subject; they do not clearly express the same or conflicting constraint.

evidence_reg: Where personal data have not been obtained from the data subject, the controller shall provide the data subject with the following information: (d) the categories of personal data concerned;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA addresses data breach notification, while the REG specifically concerns informing the data subject of categories of personal data concerned in an Article 14 context; no direct quote-supported mismatch is shown.
